topic: A coach has to cut a player from the team
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the locker room felt thick, suffocating. I watched Mark walk toward the door, his shoulders slumped, a familiar, heavy defeat etched onto his face. He didn't look back, and I made sure my gaze stayed fixed on the chipped paint of my locker. Every time he paused, I felt a tightening in my chest, a hard knot forming just beneath my ribs. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a flush that had nothing to do with exertion. "It's time, Mark," Coach Sterling said, his voice flat, devoid of the usual booming encouragement. The words landed like stones. I clenched my jaw so hard my teeth ached, forcing a neutral expression onto my features, though inside, a cold, sharp anger was starting to prickle. I just nodded once, a jerky, mechanical movement, and turned my back to the scene, focusing intently on the scuff marks on the floor.

[story 2]

I kept drumming my fingers against the worn wooden bench, the rhythm too fast, too aggressive. When Coach finally called my name, I didn't look up immediately. I let the silence stretch, letting the weight of the moment settle entirely on him. When I finally lifted my eyes, they met his for a fraction of a second—a flicker of something hard and unyielding—before dropping again. I noticed the way his eyes lingered on the empty space where I usually stood, a brief, assessing glance that felt like a physical prod. When he spoke about the roster changes, my hands curled into tight fists inside my jeans. I felt a strange, burning sensation behind my eyes, hot and unwelcome, and I made a show of taking a slow, deep breath, trying to keep the tremor out of my voice. "Understood," I managed, the word tasting like ash on my tongue.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights hummed with an irritating, monotonous drone, mirroring the dull throb in my skull. I stood by the water cooler, pretending to examine the condensation on the plastic bottle, while every nerve ending in my body was screaming at the injustice of it all. When Coach announced the decision, I felt a sudden, intense cold wash over me, a stark contrast to the feverish heat building up inside. I could feel my knuckles turning white where I gripped the cooler. I didn't move until the announcement was over. When I finally turned, I saw the disappointment in the eyes of the other players, and instead of mirroring it, I let a slow, deliberate sigh escape my lips. It was a sound laced with a deep, bitter dissatisfaction, a quiet acknowledgment that this outcome was entirely undeserved.
